title: Whats a reasonable price for a set of Platinum DVDs
display_title: Whats a reasonable price for a set of Platinum DVDs
wikitext:
[]: What's a reasonable price for a set of Platinum DVDs?             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Tue Dec 31, 2013 7:57 pm I've been trying a find good deal on a set of Platinum DVDs, but ebay and amazon are too pricey, it also disappeared from circlered.com too and finding it seems to be a challenge. Why do some sets have 7 discs instead of 6 discs? What's the normal price range for the set? Also, which, is the best one to get? What do they normally go for?

[]:             Quote Post by Maya Ibuki » Wed Jan 01, 2014 11:03 am Evangelion TV has been long out of print - both in Japan and the Western World. These days, used sets of the Platinum version will vary in price - there's debate as to whether or not it is a 'collectors' item per-say. Honestly, my personal opinion is that the $40-60 mark would be reasonable for used copies of both platinum releases. The original platinum boxset, containing 7 discs, was housed in a box with individual volumes. These volumes include extras and booklets. The fatpack, a re-release, contains 6 discs - no extras or booklets, but a higher bitrate; the video quality is somewhat higher due to the lack of compression. It's shocking to see people listing sets for up to $100+, but I'm assuming times have changed since I last purchased any Eva material. To be honest I bought a new fatpack set a year ago for $50 at my local entertainment store in Melbourne. It seems as though it really is out of stock now - can't find them anywhere. I own all of these releases, and I must say I find the fatpack the most convenient. Nice to store and access overall - and if the rumors are true, the quality is much better than the other release.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jan 01, 2014 11:20 am Maya Ibuki wrote: $40-60 mark would be reasonable for used copies of both platinum releases

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Jan 01, 2014 11:28 am Surely one could just buy the old single releases on dvd from the early '00s. True you don't get as many episodes on a disk(compared to the platinums) but they are certainly much cheaper than spending 50 bucks + on a platinum set..

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Wed Jan 01, 2014 1:06 pm I want the thinpack, no extras, it has the director's cuts, but snagging it on ebay seems to be problem because no one ever puts it up for buy it now. I'm annoyed, I want to find it for the $40-80 price range, maybe if I was lucky I could find the holiday set of it. XD

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 01, 2014 1:14 pm Princess Asuka wrote: Ino one ever puts it up for buy it now.

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Wed Jan 01, 2014 1:26 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: You do understand that a BIN item costs more. What you want is a no reserve auction. That's how you'll get a fair price for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 01, 2014 2:45 pm I think Zap has his own copy of the Platinum Thinpack (no extras, just the episodes) that he's been meaning to sell.

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Wed Jan 01, 2014 2:56 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I think Zap has his own copy of the Platinum Thinpack (no extras, just the episodes) that he's been meaning to sell.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 01, 2014 7:21 pm Princess Asuka wrote: Yeah, I know, if people would do that, then things would go more smoothly and I could get a good deal on it.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Jan 01, 2014 7:58 pm Yeah, I've been trying to sell mine for ≈$80-90, but I've only asked a couple EGFers about it. Maybe I should just go for the auction route. I like to keep it in the community though. I also have Manga's D&R/EoE. Both in great condition.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 01, 2014 8:06 pm Monkey: Out of general curiosity, do you happen to know how much the single volume (no box, just the slipcovers) Platinums would go for, whether they'd be sold individually or as a set of 7? My Volume 5 is the only one missing the Renewal-translated booklet (though they're all transcribed on your site anyway ).

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Mon Jan 06, 2014 5:22 pm How much does the holiday edition go for? There are two currently up on ebay, one with starts at $105 with 0 bids and the other has 1 bid at $70. Also, there's the the singles of Platinum and the box with volume 1 that's brand new with no bids starting at $115. Along with a thinpack that's 150 BIN or best offer. Ugh, so many choices.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 06, 2014 5:50 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Out of general curiosity, do you happen to know how much the single volume (no box, just the slipcovers) Platinums would go for

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 06, 2014 5:58 pm I've seen the single volumes of Platinum go anywhere from $8 to $35+ on eBay, that's why I was curious how much the 7 DVDs would go for.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 06, 2014 6:10 pm It's also worth working out an intelligent strategy for getting the lowest price, especially if there's only one other serious bidder. Don't keep increasing your bid to be the front runner - that just encourages the other bidder to do the same, and pushes the price way up. Wait until the end; and then, if at the closing time the current bid is within your limit, wait until there's less than 30 seconds or so to go, and make your maximum possible bid - if you can make it late enough for them to be unable to respond, you're good. There's software to do this automatically, which I'm not sure you're officially allowed to use, but I enjoy the fun of doing it manually - it helps to have a fast and reliable Internet connection, of course. Equally, don't get carried away by the thrill of winning - I'm amazed how often auctions go to a higher price than currently available buy-it-now offers! Also, if there is a "best offer" around at a considerably higher price, but a similar item goes at a lower price at auction, offering the recent auction price will often get the item for you.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jan 06, 2014 11:23 pm ^I agree, tipping your hand too early generally isn't good. I too like to snipe - came up with the term independently but it exists on Wikipedia. You could also bid to jack up the price and make someone else pay more as long as you don't get caught holding the bag, but why cultivate negative karma.
